metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state in PostgreSQL and ClickHouse, exposes full CRUD operations for users, websites, teams, links, pixels, and reports through REST API endpoints, and manages entity lifecycle with soft deletes.

evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered. No operations >30s detected.
Extended question not triggered. No external service dependencies detected.
Extended question not triggered. Service is P2, not on critical path.

findings.title: No Documented API Interface
findings.description: The application exposes 70+ REST API route handlers under src/app/api/ implemented as Next.js App Router route handlers. No formal documentation, no OpenAPI specification, no endpoint catalog exists.
findings.gap: No documented API interface. Integration requires source code reverse-engineering.
findings.recommendation: Generate an OpenAPI 3.x specification from existing routes and Zod schemas using tools like zod-to-openapi or next-swagger-doc.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Generate an OpenAPI 3.x specification from existing route handlers and Zod schemas.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Machine-readable OpenAPI specification documenting all API endpoints, schemas, auth requirements, and error responses, kept in sync via CI validation.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The application supports bearer token authentication via encrypted JWTs for human users. No OAuth2 client credentials flow, no API key with principal attribution, no mTLS, no service account concept exists.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication. Agents must authenticate using human-style username/password login. No way to distinguish agent principals from human principals.
findings.recommendation: Implement API key authentication with per-key principal attribution, or add an OAuth2 client credentials flow for machine-to-machine access.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Implement API key authentication with per-key principal attribution for agent identities.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Machine identity authentication that distinguishes agent principals from human principals in audit logs, with per-agent API keys or OAuth2 client credentials.

findings.title: Coarse-Grained Permissions
findings.description: Role-based system exists (admin, user, view-only, team roles) but is coarse-grained. No mechanism for scoping an agent identity to a subset of operations or specific resources.
findings.gap: No fine-grained per-resource permission scoping. Agent identities inherit full role permissions without restriction.
findings.recommendation: Create a dedicated agent role with minimal permissions or leverage existing view-only role for read-only agent access.

compensating_controls: Create a dedicated agent role with restricted permissions
Use the existing view-only role for read-only agent access

findings.title: Limited Action-Level Authorization for Agent Identities
findings.description: Action-level permission functions exist (canView, canUpdate, canDelete) but the user role bundles all CRUD permissions together.
findings.gap: Cannot grant read-without-write access at the user role level for owned resources.
findings.recommendation: Use view-only or team-view-only roles for agent identities to restrict to read operations.

compensating_controls: Assign agent identities the view-only role
Implement API Gateway-level method restrictions (allow GET only) for agent traffic

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: Application uses debug library for diagnostics. No structured audit logging recording principal, action, resource, or timestamp for API operations.
findings.gap: No audit trail for agent or human actions. Cannot determine who performed which operation.
findings.recommendation: Introduce audit logging middleware in parseRequest() that logs authenticated user ID, HTTP method, endpoint path, and timestamp to a structured append-only log store.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, evaluated below BLOCKER threshold.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not perform write operations, so immutable audit logging is important but not a deployment blocker.
compensating_controls: Implement request-level access logging at reverse proxy layer

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: Soft-delete exists but checkAuth() does not validate deletedAt. JWT tokens remain valid regardless of user state. No token revocation.
findings.gap: Cannot immediately suspend an agent identity and invalidate its active sessions.
findings.recommendation: Add deletedAt check in checkAuth(). Implement Redis-based token blacklist or reduce token TTL.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting or Throttling
findings.description: No rate limiting middleware, API gateway throttling, or WAF rate rules. Endpoints can be called at unlimited speed.
findings.gap: No rate limiting at any layer. Agent or attacker traffic could overwhelm the application.
findings.recommendation: Implement rate limiting middleware using Redis as backing store or deploy behind an API gateway with per-client throttling.

compensating_controls: Deploy behind reverse proxy with rate limiting

findings.title: Insufficient Access Control Differentiation for Sensitive Data
findings.description: System stores user passwords (bcrypt hashed), usernames, and IP-derived geolocation. Passwords properly excluded from API responses. However, no differentiation between sensitive and non-sensitive data access scopes.
findings.gap: B2 finding: No access control differentiation. All authenticated users access the same analytics data without scope-based restrictions.
findings.recommendation: Implement OAuth scopes or fine-grained permissions that distinguish access levels for analytics data vs user management data.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, B1 is CLEAR (passwords excluded), B2 fires as RISK-SAFETY.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q1 B1 is CLEAR because passwords are properly excluded from API responses. B2 fires because there is no access differentiation between sensitive data domains. Overall DATA-Q1 severity is RISK-SAFETY (highest sub-check that fires).

b1.reasoning: Passwords excluded from API responses via explicit select/omit patterns.

b2.reasoning: No differentiation between sensitive and non-sensitive data access scopes. All authenticated users access same analytics data.

b3.reasoning: No formal data classification tags or field-level sensitivity annotations.

compensating_controls: Assign agent identities view-only role limiting scope
Implement per-team data isolation for agent access

findings.title: PII Exposed in Debug Logs
findings.description: Debug logging outputs auth tokens and user objects without redaction. Kafka error handler serializes full errors which may contain request context.
findings.gap: No PII masking in logs when debug mode is active.
findings.recommendation: Remove or redact sensitive fields from debug log statements. Replace full object logging with sanitized summaries.

compensating_controls: Ensure DEBUG environment variable is not set in production
Add log filtering at infrastructure level

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or equivalent specification exists. API is code-first with inline Zod schemas.
findings.gap: Agent tool generation requires manual authoring from source code inspection.
findings.recommendation: Auto-generate OpenAPI 3.x specification from Zod schemas using zod-to-openapi.

findings.title: Error Responses Lack Retryable Classification
findings.description: Consistent {error: {message, code, status}} structure exists but missing retryable field to distinguish retriable from terminal errors.
findings.gap: Agents cannot programmatically distinguish retriable errors from terminal errors.
findings.recommendation: Add retryable: boolean field to error response schema.

findings.title: Credentials in Environment Variables Without Secrets Manager
findings.description: APP_SECRET via environment variable. docker-compose.yml contains hardcoded placeholder credentials. No secrets manager integration.
findings.gap: No secrets management system. No rotation mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Integrate AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault for production deployments.

findings.title: No Production-Equivalent Staging Environment
findings.description: Docker Compose testing environment exists with seed data but is not production-equivalent. No documented staging environment.
findings.gap: Testing environment not production-equivalent in scale or data shape.
findings.recommendation: Document staging environment setup. Enhance seed data generators for production-scale data.

findings.title: No API Versioning or Contract Testing
findings.description: No API versioning scheme, no breaking change detection in CI. 14 Prisma migrations document schema evolution but no API contract stability guarantee.
findings.gap: Agent tool bindings could break silently on any deployment.
findings.recommendation: Introduce URL-based API versioning and breaking-change detection in CI.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or structured logging. Only debug library for unstructured diagnostic output.
findings.gap: Agent-initiated requests cannot be traced through the system.
findings.recommendation: Integrate OpenTelemetry SDK with structured JSON logging and correlation IDs.

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration, or anomaly detection. Basic Docker healthcheck only.
findings.gap: No operational alerting for API degradation affecting agents.
findings.recommendation: Implement metrics collection and alerting thresholds for error rates and latency.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, or Kubernetes manifests. Deployment via Docker Compose and PaaS configs only.
findings.gap: Agent-facing infrastructure not governed by code, review, or drift detection.
findings.recommendation: Define deployment infrastructure as code including networking, secrets, and observability.

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: CI runs tests and build. No API contract testing, consumer-driven contracts, or breaking change detection.
findings.gap: API changes not validated against contracts before production.
findings.recommendation: Add API contract tests (Pact or OpenAPI diff) to CI pipeline.

findings.title: Manual Rollback Only
findings.description: Versioned Docker images provide basic rollback. No automated triggers, no canary deployment, no blue/green. Database migrations are forward-only.
findings.gap: Rollback requires manual intervention. No automated rollback on error rate increase.
findings.recommendation: Implement automated rollback triggers and database migration rollback scripts.

findings.title: Minimal API Test Coverage
findings.description: 3 unit tests and 6 E2E tests for 70+ API route handlers. Vast majority of agent-facing endpoints untested.
findings.gap: Most API endpoints lack automated test coverage.
findings.recommendation: Expand Cypress API test suite to cover all agent-facing endpoints.

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest Configuration
findings.description: No encryption-at-rest configuration defined. PostgreSQL in docker-compose uses defaults. Depends on deployment platform.
findings.gap: No explicit encryption guarantee for stored data.
findings.recommendation: Use managed database services with encryption at rest enabled.
